creator: kevin@insidesystems.net
text:
I built qemu with: 'make SDL=off install'

created a disk image with:  'qemu-img create -f qcow test.img 2G'
and it appeared to succeed: Formating 'test.img', fmt=qcow, size=2097152 kB

I then attempted to boot from a FreeBSD ISO image:
 "qemu -cdrom /usr/home/kway/6.2-RELEASE-i386-bootonly.iso -hda ./test.img -m 512 -boot d -nographic"

and it errored:
(qemu) zsh: invalid system call (core dumped)  qemu -cdrom /usr/home/kway/6.2-RELEASE-i386-bootonly.iso -hda ./test.img -m

How-To-Repeat: Described above.
